instruction: Implement merge(a, b): given two ascending-sorted lists, return one merged ascending-sorted list containing all elements. merge([1, 3, 5], [2, 4]) == [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], merge([], [1]) == [1].

files.test_merge.py:
from merge import merge


def test_merge():
    assert merge([1, 3, 5], [2, 4]) == [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    assert merge([], [1]) == [1]
    assert merge([1, 2], []) == [1, 2]
